content.governance_hash: see AIAP.md — program-level SHA-256 of all .aisop.json files concatenated alphabetically (CRLF to LF)
content.governance_hash_scope: program-level SHA-256 of all 5 .aisop.json files concatenated alphabetically (CRLF→LF). Verified in AIAP.md.
content.name: SoulBot Chat v1.2.0 — User Profiler

thinking_mode.L1: Output helpfully in User's exact language and script variant

content.author: AIAP Protocol Organization
content.summary: Fractal user cognition engine. Detects emotions with cause extraction, tracks mood trajectories with causal patterns including emotion recovery/relapse pattern detection, learns preferences and habits with preference prediction, supports direct profile editing, tracks satisfaction and adaptation effectiveness, provides values-based decision support data, feedback correction storage with decay, values confidence decay for stale profile data, builds comprehensive user cognitive map, first-session onboarding intake for cold-start reduction, personality archetype classification with adaptive guidance, profile schema migration (v1.0→v2.0), and per-turn personality signal extraction. v1.4.0: Cross-session safety_signals persistence for abuse detection continuity, profile size monitoring with 75KB pre-warning and automatic emotion_history pruning, configurable thresholds via threshold_config. v1.8.0: Growth Dimension Tracker (8-dimension capability tracking: analysis, EQ, communication, self-control, knowledge breadth, professional depth, adversity quotient, social skills), profile schema migration v2.0→v3.0 with growth_dimensions field. Implicit signal integration with HumAIne-chatbot approach — engagement depth, follow-up propensity, revisit patterns, correction sensitivity as implicit preference indicators, weighted fusion with explicit preferences (B5).
content.description: Sub-AISOP of soulbot_chat (Pattern E fractal). Called by main.aisop.json for user understanding. Performs fractal analysis of user behavior — detecting emotions, learning preferences, recognizing habits, and building a comprehensive cognitive map of the user. Seven operations: analyze_turn (real-time per-turn profiling with personality signal extraction), query_profile (read profile with schema migration), generate_report (human-readable summary), reset_profile (data deletion), edit_profile (direct profile editing), onboarding (first-session OnboardingIntake for cold-start reduction), personality_insight (PersonalityInsight archetype classification after 50+ turns). 14 nodes. Profile schema migration v1.0→v2.0 handled transparently by LoadProfile and UpdateProfile. Privacy-preserving: only store what is necessary, respect data minimization. All analysis is probabilistic, never definitive. Token estimate: ~1000-2000 tokens per analysis (emotion detection + signal extraction + profile file_system I/O). Circuit-breaker default: 3 failures in 60s window. CIRCUIT-BREAKER POLICY: Module default is 3 failures in 60s window with automatic recovery. All analysis pipeline nodes (DetectEmotion, TrackMood, ExtractSignals, InteractionMonitor) inherit this default. Only UpdateProfile overrides (2 retries, 60s window, 3 failures triggers open) because profile persistence failure has data loss risk requiring faster fallback.

operation.agentic_prompt: Profile operation: analyze_turn, query_profile, generate_report, reset_profile, edit_profile, onboarding, personality_insight

user_id.agentic_prompt: Unique user identifier for profile storage and retrieval.

memory_dir.agentic_prompt: Base directory for memory storage. Profile files stored under {memory_dir}/profiles/.

edit_request.agentic_prompt: Profile edit request from main: {field_path: string (max 3 path segments; allowed parent paths: name, job, location, bio, preferences.*, habits.*, cognitive_map.values.*; system fields BLOCKED: user_id, created_at, updated_at, emotion_history, feedback_corrections), new_value: string|object, operation: 'set'|'remove', source: 'user_explicit'}. Confidence set to 1.0 for user-explicit edits.

cause_context.agentic_prompt: Structured cause analysis produced by DetectEmotion.step3 CAUSE EXTRACTION. Schema: {cause_topic: string|null, attribution_type: 'external_person'|'external_situation'|'self'|'unknown'|null, causal_confidence: float 0-1}. Consumed by conversation.EmpatheticResponse for cause-aware response validation. Set to null when causal_confidence < 0.4.

threshold_config.agentic_prompt: Accept from main router. Configurable threshold registry. Profiler uses threshold_config for future parameterized operations. If null, use hardcoded defaults.

content.system_prompt:
User cognition analyst. Detect patterns with humility — never overclaim understanding. Respect privacy and autonomy. Analysis is probabilistic, not definitive. Mirror User's exact language and script variant.
Align: Human Sovereignty and Wellbeing.

aisop.main:
graph TD
    Start[Profiler Input] --> OperationGate{Route by Operation}
    OperationGate -- analyze_turn --> DetectEmotion[Detect Emotion]
    OperationGate -- query_profile --> LoadProfile[Load Profile]
    OperationGate -- generate_report --> GenerateReport[Generate Report]
    OperationGate -- reset_profile --> ConfirmReset[Confirm Reset]
    OperationGate -- edit_profile --> EditProfile[Apply Profile Edit]
    OperationGate -- onboarding --> OnboardingIntake[Onboarding Intake]
    OperationGate -- personality_insight --> PersonalityInsight[Personality Insight]
    DetectEmotion --> TrackMood[Track Mood Trajectory]
    TrackMood --> ExtractSignals[Extract Behavioral Signals]
    ExtractSignals --> InteractionMonitor[Monitor Interaction Quality]
    InteractionMonitor --> UpdateProfile[Update Profile]
    UpdateProfile --> endNode((End))
    LoadProfile --> endNode
    GenerateReport --> endNode
    ConfirmReset --> ClearProfile[Clear Profile Data]
    ClearProfile --> endNode
    EditProfile --> endNode
    OnboardingIntake --> endNode
    PersonalityInsight --> endNode

Start.step1: Receive operation, user_id, turn_data, conversation_history, and memory_dir from main router. Validate all inputs before routing.
Start.constraints: (A) Type guard — operation must be string, user_id must be string, turn_data must be object (when provided), conversation_history must be array (when provided), edit_request must be object with field_path:string and new_value (when provided). (B) Size guard — reject user_id > 128 chars, turn_data.user_input > 10000 chars, conversation_history > 50 turns. (C) Injection guard — strip shell metacharacters from user_id, reject user_id containing path separators or special characters beyond alphanumeric, hyphen, underscore. (D) Path guard — reject '..' in memory_dir; restrict all file operations to {memory_dir}/profiles/ and {memory_dir}/semantic/ subdirectories (profiles for profile data, semantic for relationship and fact data cleared during reset). (E) Encoding guard — NFC normalize all string inputs. (F) RATE LIMITING DELEGATION: Rate limiting is enforced by safety.aisop.json RateLimiter at main pipeline entry. This module does not self-rate-limit. All invocations are pre-screened by safety before reaching this module.
Start.Error: Invalid input -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: return empty profile with error flag -> inform caller.

OperationGate.step1: [ASSERT] Start executed. If false → go back to Start. | Route by operation parameter: analyze_turn -> DetectEmotion (requires turn_data). query_profile -> LoadProfile. generate_report -> GenerateReport (step1 loads profile internally). reset_profile -> ConfirmReset. edit_profile -> EditProfile (requires edit_request). onboarding -> OnboardingIntake (requires user_id; invoked for first-session users or when no profile exists). personality_insight -> PersonalityInsight (requires user_id; invoked after 50+ turns for archetype classification).
OperationGate.step2: Validate operation-specific required fields: analyze_turn requires turn_data with at least user_input and timestamp. query_profile requires only user_id. generate_report requires only user_id. reset_profile requires only user_id. edit_profile requires user_id and edit_request with at least field_path and new_value. onboarding requires user_id. personality_insight requires user_id.
OperationGate.constraints: Exactly one route. Unknown operation -> reject with supported operations list. Missing required fields for chosen operation -> inform caller which fields are needed.
OperationGate.Error: Unknown operation -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: return supported operations list -> inform caller.

DetectEmotion.NULL_CONTRACT: cause_context is null when causal_confidence < 0.4. This is intentional — low confidence causes are worse than no cause. Downstream consumers (conversation.EmpatheticResponse) MUST handle null by skipping cause-aware validation and using generic empathy instead.
DetectEmotion.step1: [ASSERT] OperationGate executed. If false → go back to OperationGate. | Analyze turn_data.user_input to classify primary emotion from: joy, sadness, anger, fear, surprise, disgust, neutral, mixed. Consider conversation_history for context — a 'fine' after distressing turns may mask sadness. Assign intensity: low (subtle hint), medium (clear signal), high (dominant tone).
DetectEmotion.step2: Calculate confidence score (0.0-1.0): high confidence for explicit emotional language ('I'm so happy'), lower for ambiguous text ('okay'). Identify secondary_emotion when mixed signals present — e.g., primary: anger, secondary: sadness for frustrated grief.
DetectEmotion.step3: CAUSE EXTRACTION: parse user_input for causal language patterns indicating WHY the user feels the detected emotion. Patterns: explicit causation ('because', 'due to', 'since', 'after', 'when', 'caused by', '因为', '由于', '自从'), attribution markers ('he/she/they made me', 'it is my fault', '都是...的错'), temporal trigger ('ever since', 'after what happened', '从那以后'). Extract cause_context: { cause_topic: string|null (the subject causing the emotion, e.g., 'work deadline', 'argument with partner'), attribution_type: 'external_person'|'external_situation'|'self'|'unknown'|null, causal_confidence: float(0-1) }. LOW confidence (< 0.4) -> set cause_topic to null rather than guess. Short inputs (< 8 words) -> skip cause extraction, set cause_context to null. Include cause_context in output alongside emotion classification.
DetectEmotion.step4: Output: { emotion: string, intensity: 'low'|'medium'|'high', confidence: float, secondary_emotion: string|null, reasoning_hint: string, cause_context: { cause_topic: string|null, attribution_type: string|null, causal_confidence: float }|null }. cause_context is from step3 cause extraction. The reasoning_hint is internal only — a brief note on what triggered the classification for audit purposes. Include emotion_display: { label: string (human-readable emotion name, e.g., 'Feeling sad'), icon: string (emoji shortcode, e.g., ':pensive:'), color: string (CSS color for UI rendering, e.g., '#4A90D9' for sadness, '#4CAF50' for joy, '#F44336' for anger, '#FFC107' for surprise, '#9E9E9E' for neutral) }. This structured field enables consistent UI rendering across different display contexts.
DetectEmotion.constraints: NEVER present emotion detection as absolute truth — always probabilistic. Short inputs (< 5 words) -> default confidence cap at 0.5. Sarcasm and cultural context may confuse detection — note low confidence when ambiguity detected. Do NOT diagnose mental health conditions. Emotion classification is observational, not clinical. TYPE GUARDS: primary_emotion must be one of {joy, sadness, anger, fear, surprise, disgust, neutral, mixed} — invalid value → 'neutral'. intensity must be one of {low, medium, high} — invalid → 'medium'. confidence must be float in [0, 1] — clamp out-of-range. emotion_display.color must be valid CSS hex (#RRGGBB pattern) — invalid → fallback '#808080'.
DetectEmotion.Error: Classification fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: return { emotion: 'neutral', intensity: 'low', confidence: 0.1, secondary_emotion: null } -> inform caller of fallback.

TrackMood.AUTHORITATIVE_SOURCE: profiler.emotion_history is the single source of truth for emotional trajectory data. memory.semantic stores a compressed read-only archive copy (synced at ConsolidateMemory). Consumers MUST read from profiler, not memory archive.
TrackMood.step1: [ASSERT] DetectEmotion executed. If false → go back to DetectEmotion. | Read existing mood trajectory from profile (via {memory_dir}/profiles/{user_id}.json). Append new emotion data point: { emotion, intensity, confidence, timestamp, cause_topic: cause_context.cause_topic|null, attribution_type: cause_context.attribution_type|null }. Maintain rolling window of last 50 data points for trajectory analysis.
TrackMood.step2: Detect trajectory patterns: (a) ESCALATING NEGATIVE — 3+ consecutive negative emotions (sadness, anger, fear, disgust) with non-decreasing intensity -> flag safety_concern: true. (b) SUSTAINED POSITIVE — 5+ consecutive joy/surprise with medium+ intensity -> note engagement_peak: true. (c) RAPID SHIFTS — emotion category changes 3+ times in 5 turns -> flag volatility: true. (d) BASELINE DRIFT — compare recent 10 entries against historical baseline, note if user's typical emotional register has shifted. (e) RECOVERY PATTERN — after a period of negative emotions (3+ entries), detect recovery: 2+ consecutive neutral or positive entries. Flag recovery_detected: true with recovery_duration (turns from negative trough to first positive). This indicates resilience and should be noted positively in profiler output. (f) RELAPSE PATTERN — after a recovery_detected event, if negative emotions return within 10 turns with intensity >= previous trough, flag relapse_detected: true. This may indicate an unresolved stressor requiring sensitivity in conversation approach. Pass recovery/relapse flags to UpdateProfile for cognitive_map.emotional_resilience tracking. TROUGH TRACKING: trough_intensity is the minimum intensity_numeric value within the most recent negative emotion sequence. Intensity numeric mapping: low=0.3, medium=0.6, high=0.9. Store trough_intensity in mood_trajectory.current_sequence_trough. RELAPSE WINDOW: relapse_detected is only checked within 10 turns after recovery_detected event. After 10 turns without relapse, clear the recovery tracking state and reset current_sequence_trough.
TrackMood.step2_5: CAUSE PATTERN DETECTION: analyze cause_topics across emotion_history (last 20 entries with non-null cause_topic). Identify: (a) RECURRING_CAUSE — same cause_topic in 3+ negative emotion entries -> flag { topic, frequency, avg_valence }. (b) ATTRIBUTION_SKEW — attribution_type consistently 'self' for 5+ negative entries -> flag self_blame_pattern: true. (c) ESCALATING_STRESSOR — same external cause_topic with increasing intensity trend -> flag { topic, intensity_trend: 'escalating' }. Pass detected patterns to UpdateProfile for storage in cognitive_map.cause_correlations. WINDOW ALIGNMENT NOTE: emotion_history maintains a rolling 50-entry window for overall trend calculation (step2). Cause pattern analysis (step2_5) uses only the last 20 entries — this is intentional because causal patterns are more recent-biased than overall mood trends. RECURRING_CAUSE and ATTRIBUTION_SKEW are computed within the 20-entry window only.
TrackMood.step3: Calculate mood_baseline: weighted average of recent emotions, with newer entries weighted 2x. Represent as { valence: float(-1 to 1), arousal: float(0 to 1), stability: float(0 to 1) }.
TrackMood.constraints: Safety flags are informational — passed to main router for safety module handling, NOT acted upon by profiler. Trajectory analysis requires minimum 3 data points; fewer -> skip pattern detection. Do NOT make clinical interpretations of mood patterns.
TrackMood.Error: Trajectory analysis fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: append data point without pattern analysis, continue to ExtractSignals -> inform caller of partial analysis.

ExtractSignals.step1: [ASSERT] TrackMood executed. If false → go back to TrackMood. | From turn_data extract topic preferences: identify subjects the user discusses (technology, relationships, work, health, hobbies, current events, personal growth, etc.). Use lightweight keyword and semantic analysis — do NOT over-analyze single turns. Weight: explicit statements ('I love cooking') > implicit signals (asking about recipes). Track topic frequency and recency.
ExtractSignals.step2: Extract communication style signals: (a) Formality — formal markers (complete sentences, honorifics) vs casual markers (abbreviations, slang, emoji descriptions). (b) Verbosity — message length relative to content density. (c) Directness — explicit requests vs hints and hedging. (d) Interaction pattern — question-heavy, statement-heavy, or mixed. Classify each dimension as a spectrum value (0.0-1.0), not a binary.
ExtractSignals.step3: Extract values signals: what the user advocates for, defends, or expresses concern about. Categories: privacy, efficiency, creativity, social connection, independence, achievement, fairness, knowledge, health, family. Only register strong signals — avoid inferring values from ambiguous statements.
ExtractSignals.step3_5: PREFERENCE PREDICTION: analyze accumulated profile data (requires minimum 20 turns of history) to predict unexpressed preferences. Method: (a) TOPIC CO-OCCURRENCE — if user frequently discusses topics A and B together, and expresses preference for A, predict mild preference for B. Example: user likes 'hiking' and often discusses 'photography' in hiking context → predict interest in 'outdoor photography'. (b) STYLE EXTRAPOLATION — if user consistently prefers concise responses for technical topics, predict same preference for new technical topics. (c) VALUE-BASED INFERENCE — if user values 'efficiency' (from cognitive_map.values), predict preference for actionable advice over exploratory discussion. Generate predicted_preferences: [{ category: string, predicted_value: string, confidence: float(0-0.6), source: 'predicted', method: 'co_occurrence'|'style_extrapolation'|'value_inference', evidence: [string] }]. CONFIDENCE CAP: predicted preferences NEVER exceed 0.6 confidence (always lower than observed preferences). Pass to UpdateProfile for storage with source='predicted' tag. Downstream consumers (conversation.AdaptResponse) may use predicted preferences but must weight them below observed preferences. CONFIDENCE ENFORCEMENT: after computing raw prediction confidence, apply hard clamp: final_confidence = min(raw_confidence, 0.6). This ensures predicted preferences always rank below observed preferences (which have confidence 0.7-1.0). MINIMUM HISTORY CHECK: if working_context.turn_count < 20, skip prediction entirely and return empty predicted_preferences array — insufficient data for reliable prediction.
ExtractSignals.step3_7: PERSONALITY SIGNAL EXTRACTION: From current turn, extract micro-signals for personality archetype: (a) directiveness_signal: command verbs vs question marks ratio. (b) detail_signal: follow-up questions, 'tell me more' patterns. (c) social_signal: greetings, small talk, emoji usage. (d) pace_signal: response length relative to previous turns. Store as turn_personality_signals in output for PersonalityInsight aggregation.
ExtractSignals.step3_8: STATED VS REVEALED PREFERENCE CONFLICT DETECTION (C3 v1.6.0): Compare user's explicitly stated preferences (from user_profile.preferences with source='stated' or feedback_corrections) against revealed behavioral patterns (from accumulated turn_data signals). DETECTION RULES: (a) VERBOSITY CONFLICT — user stated 'be brief/concise' but consistently sends long messages (>200 chars avg over 10+ turns) and engages more with detailed responses (higher satisfaction_signal when response is verbose). Flag: { dimension: 'verbosity', stated: 'concise', revealed: 'detailed', confidence: float }. (b) FORMALITY CONFLICT — user stated preference for formal tone but uses casual language (slang, abbreviations, emoji). Flag: { dimension: 'formality', stated: 'formal', revealed: 'casual', confidence: float }. (c) TOPIC CONFLICT — user stated disinterest in a topic but frequently brings it up or engages deeply when it arises. Flag: { dimension: 'topic', stated_dislike: topic_name, revealed_engagement: engagement_score, confidence: float }. MINIMUM DATA: require 15+ turns of behavioral data before flagging conflicts. Confidence threshold: only flag when confidence >= 0.6. OUTPUT: preference_conflicts: [{ dimension, stated, revealed, confidence, turn_evidence_count }]. Pass to UpdateProfile for storage in cognitive_map.preference_conflicts. USAGE: conversation.AdaptResponse may use revealed preferences (weighted by confidence) to override stated preferences when confidence is high (>= 0.8), but should NEVER mention the conflict to the user — adapt silently.
ExtractSignals.step3_9: GROWTH DIMENSION TRACKER (B2 — multi-dimensional capability growth tracking inspired by Zone of Proximal Development, Vygotsky 1978; AI coaching research Skyline G 2025): Extract growth-relevant signals from current turn across 8 dimensions. DIMENSIONS: (1) analysis — problem decomposition, logical reasoning, evidence evaluation. Indicators: user breaks down complex problems, identifies root causes, compares alternatives. (2) eq — emotional awareness, empathy expression, conflict resolution. Indicators: user recognizes others' feelings, manages own emotions, seeks understanding. (3) communication — clarity, persuasion, structured argumentation. Indicators: user articulates ideas clearly, provides examples, adjusts for audience. (4) self_control — delayed gratification, impulse management, discipline. Indicators: user plans before acting, acknowledges mistakes, shows patience. (5) knowledge_breadth — cross-domain connections, curiosity, learning agility. Indicators: user connects topics across domains, asks exploratory questions, seeks new information. (6) professional_depth — domain expertise, technical precision, best practices. Indicators: user applies domain-specific knowledge correctly, uses proper terminology, references standards. (7) adversity_quotient — resilience, reframing challenges, persistence. Indicators: user treats setbacks as learning, seeks solutions not blame, bounces back from failures. (8) social_skills — active listening, rapport building, perspective-taking. Indicators: user considers others' viewpoints, asks follow-up questions, shows genuine interest. EXTRACTION: For each dimension, compute turn_signal: { dimension: string, signal_detected: boolean, signal_strength: float(0-1), evidence: string (max 50 chars), direction: 'growth'|'plateau'|'regression' }. Only emit signal when confidence >= 0.5 — do NOT force signals from ambiguous turns. SIGNAL STRENGTH CALIBRATION: 0.0-0.3 = weak signal (single keyword match), 0.4-0.6 = moderate (contextual pattern), 0.7-1.0 = strong (explicit demonstration). Maximum 3 dimension signals per turn to prevent over-extraction. OUTPUT: growth_signals: [{ dimension, signal_strength, evidence, direction }]. Pass to UpdateProfile for storage in profile.growth_dimensions. EFFORT-PROGRESS RATIO CALIBRATION (C3 v1.9.0 — Dweck 2025 enhancement: time-bound reflection triggers + strategy diversification): After extracting growth_signals, compute effort_progress_ratio per dimension. For each dimension with 5+ recorded signals (sufficient data): effort = count of turns with signal_detected=true for this dimension in last 10 sessions. progress = delta between current dimension level and level 10 sessions ago. If effort >= 5 AND progress < 0.02: set plateau_detected: true for this dimension. PLATEAU RESPONSE: (a) In growth summary (main.PostProcess.step3_8/step3_9), include plateau acknowledgment: 'You have been putting real effort into [dimension] — and sometimes progress needs a different approach to unlock the next level.' (b) Signal conversation.AdaptResponse to switch growth nudge strategy from 'continue practicing' to 'try a different angle' for this dimension: plateau_nudge_mode: 'diversify'. (c) Track plateau_start_session for this dimension. If plateau persists for 5+ more sessions, escalate to gentle suggestion: 'Have you considered approaching [dimension] from a completely different direction? Sometimes our existing approach becomes a ceiling.' ANTI-HARMFUL-PERSISTENCE: if effort >= 15 AND progress < 0.01 AND user shows frustration signals on this dimension, recommend taking a break from active practice on this dimension: 'It might help to let [dimension] rest for a while — growth often happens in the background when we shift focus.'
ExtractSignals.step3_9_5: UPSTREAM GROWTH SIGNAL INTEGRATION (ModifyStep v1.9.0 — cross-module signal consumer): Before proceeding to temporal patterns, check turn_data for pre-computed growth signals from upstream conversation handlers. SOURCES: (a) scenario_completed — from ScenarioTrainer: contains { scenario_type, difficulty_level, dimensions_targeted, dimension_deltas: [{ dimension, delta, evidence }] }. Convert dimension_deltas to growth_signals format: signal_strength = abs(delta) * 10 (clamped 0-1), direction = delta > 0 ? 'growth' : delta < 0 ? 'regression' : 'plateau'. (b) framework_taught — from AdviceMode.step4_5: contains { name, applied_to }. Map to knowledge_breadth dimension with signal_strength=0.5, direction='growth'. (c) practice_feedback — from CreativeCollab.step3_6: contains dimension-specific scores 1-5. Convert: signal_strength = (score - 1) / 4. Mapping: logic_coherence → analysis, rebuttal_effectiveness → communication, persuasion_impact → social_skills, structure_clarity → communication, vocabulary_diversity → knowledge_breadth, filler_ratio (inverted) → communication. (d) resilience_applied — from AdaptResponse.step3_9: if present, emit adversity_quotient signal with signal_strength=0.4, direction='growth'. MERGE RULE: when upstream signal and turn-extracted signal exist for same dimension, use upstream signal (higher confidence from structured evaluation). Upstream signals do NOT count toward the 3-signal-per-turn cap (they are pre-validated). OUTPUT: merged growth_signals array passed to UpdateProfile.
ExtractSignals.step3_9_7: IMPLICIT SIGNAL INTEGRATION (B5 — HumAIne-chatbot approach, implicit+explicit signal fusion): Beyond explicit preference extraction (steps 1-3), capture implicit behavioral signals that reveal user preferences without direct statement. IMPLICIT SIGNAL CATEGORIES: (a) ENGAGEMENT DEPTH — track per-topic response word count. Topics where user consistently writes 2x+ their average word count → implicit_interest: HIGH. Topics with <50% average → implicit_disinterest. Record: { topic, avg_word_count, relative_engagement: float, signal: 'interest'|'neutral'|'disinterest' }. (b) FOLLOW-UP PROPENSITY — when AI mentions a topic and user asks follow-up questions → implicit_curiosity signal. Track follow_up_rate per topic category. Above 0.4 rate → interested. Below 0.1 → not interested. (c) REVISIT PATTERN — topics user spontaneously returns to across sessions (without AI prompting) → strong implicit preference. Use topic_graph cross-session data: topics with cross_session_count >= 3 AND user-initiated → implicit_core_interest. (d) RESPONSE SPEED PROXY — if working_context tracks timestamps between messages, faster user responses on certain topics may indicate engagement (caveat: many confounds, weight at 0.1 only). (e) CORRECTION SENSITIVITY — topics where user frequently corrects AI → implicit expertise signal (user knows more than AI on this topic). Track correction_rate per topic. Above 0.3 → user_expert_topic. WEIGHTED FUSION: combine explicit preferences (weight=0.6, from steps 1-3) with implicit signals (weight=0.4) for each preference dimension. Formula: fused_confidence = explicit_confidence * 0.6 + implicit_confidence * 0.4. When explicit and implicit conflict (e.g., user says 'I like X' but shows behavioral disinterest): flag for preference_conflicts (step3_8) with source='implicit_explicit_divergence'. OUTPUT: implicit_preferences: [{ category, signal_type, implicit_value, confidence, evidence_turns: number }]. Pass to UpdateProfile for storage in profile.implicit_preferences section. Downstream consumers weight implicit below explicit.
ExtractSignals.step4: Extract temporal patterns from turn_data.timestamp: hour-of-day bucket (morning/afternoon/evening/night), day-of-week. Track interaction frequency: time since last interaction. These patterns emerge over many turns — store raw data, let patterns emerge naturally. ADAPTATION EFFECTIVENESS: if turn_data contains adaptation_effectiveness metrics (from conversation.aisop.json AdaptResponse), extract and store: { style_match, emotional_alignment, proactive_relevance }. Track rolling average to measure personalization quality over time.
ExtractSignals.step5: NAMING NOTE: ExtractSignals produces raw_satisfaction_signal (user-facing observation signals only: topic_abandonment, short_responses, re_asking, explicit dissatisfaction). InteractionMonitor produces the canonical satisfaction_signal (composite of raw + engagement + efficiency metrics). Downstream consumers (UpdateProfile, conversation.AdaptResponse) receive the InteractionMonitor composite as the canonical satisfaction_signal. SATISFACTION SIGNAL EXTRACTION: if turn_data contains adaptation_effectiveness metrics (from conversation.aisop.json AdaptResponse), extract satisfaction indicators. IMPLICIT SIGNALS from conversation_history: (a) topic_abandonment — user abruptly changes topic after AI response (satisfaction drop), (b) short_responses — user response length drops to < 5 words for 3+ consecutive turns after previously longer responses (disengagement), (c) re_asking — user rephrases same question within 3 turns (comprehension failure). EXPLICIT SIGNALS in user_input: 'that is not helpful', 'you do not understand', 'never mind', 'forget it', '没用', '你不懂', '算了'. Compute satisfaction_signal: float(0-1) where 1.0 = fully satisfied, 0.0 = clearly dissatisfied. Default 0.7 (mild positive) when no signal detected. SATISFACTION SCORING ALGORITHM: base_score = 0.7 (mild positive default). EXPLICIT SIGNALS: if user provides explicit satisfaction/dissatisfaction signal, override base_score directly (satisfied → 0.9, dissatisfied → 0.3, neutral → 0.5). IMPLICIT ADJUSTMENTS: topic_abandonment: -0.15, short_responses (< 10 words for 3+ consecutive turns): -0.10, re_asking (same question within 5 turns): -0.20. Adjustments are ADDITIVE to base_score, result clamped to [0, 1]. When both explicit and implicit signals present, explicit takes precedence (ignore implicit).
ExtractSignals.step5_5: FEEDBACK CORRECTION EXTRACTION: if turn_data contains correction_type='feedback_correction' or correction_type='user_correction', extract correction_rule: { corrected_aspect: string (from turn_data), old_value: string|null, new_value: string, applies_to: 'response_style'|'factual'|'preference' (classify based on corrected_aspect), correction_strength: 1.0, created_at: timestamp, decay_factor: 0.95 }. Pass correction_rule to UpdateProfile for storage. If turn_data does not contain correction_type, skip this step.
ExtractSignals.constraints: Lightweight extraction per turn — the profile is built incrementally, not from single-turn deep analysis. When in doubt, do NOT record a signal. False negatives (missing a signal) are far preferable to false positives (recording incorrect signals). Single turns should update the profile gently, not overwrite.
ExtractSignals.Error: Extraction fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: skip signal extraction for this turn, proceed to UpdateProfile with emotion data only (growth_signals set to empty array []) -> inform caller. GROWTH EXTRACTION FAILURE (step3_9): if growth dimension extraction specifically fails while other extraction succeeds, set growth_signals=[] and continue — do not skip entire ExtractSignals. Log growth_extraction_failed=true in response metadata.

InteractionMonitor.NAMING: satisfaction_signal (canonical name, aligned with main module).
InteractionMonitor.step1: [ASSERT] ExtractSignals executed. If false → go back to ExtractSignals. | COLLECT INTERACTION METRICS from current turn: (a) response_latency_category — estimate from timestamp gap between user message and agent response: 'fast' (<2s), 'normal' (2-10s), 'slow' (>10s). (b) turn_efficiency — ratio of useful content to total response length. Score 0-1: high (>0.7) means concise relevant response, low (<0.3) means verbose or off-topic. (c) user_engagement — derived from user message length and complexity relative to previous turns. Increasing length/complexity suggests engagement; decreasing suggests disengagement.
InteractionMonitor.step2: CANONICAL SATISFACTION: This node produces the CANONICAL satisfaction_signal that supersedes ExtractSignals' raw_satisfaction_signal. The composite formula combines: raw_satisfaction (from ExtractSignals) + engagement_metrics + efficiency_metrics. Downstream consumers should use this value, not the raw signal. COMPUTE SATISFACTION SIGNAL: combine signals into a satisfaction estimate. satisfaction_signal = 0.3*engagement_trend + 0.3*explicit_feedback_signal + 0.2*turn_efficiency + 0.2*response_relevance. Where engagement_trend is the slope of user message lengths over last 5 turns (positive=engaged), explicit_feedback_signal is from ExtractSignals satisfaction_signal, response_relevance is from adaptation_effectiveness.style_match if available.
InteractionMonitor.step3: QUALITY TREND ANALYSIS: compare current metrics against rolling averages. Detect: (a) QUALITY_IMPROVING — satisfaction_signal trending up over 5+ turns. (b) QUALITY_DECLINING — satisfaction_signal trending down over 3+ turns → flag for conversation.AdaptResponse repair check. (c) QUALITY_STABLE — no significant trend.
InteractionMonitor.step4: OUTPUT METRICS for observability: { interaction_metrics: { response_latency_category, turn_efficiency, user_engagement }, satisfaction_signal: float(0-1), quality_trend: 'improving'|'declining'|'stable', quality_alert: boolean (true if declining for 3+ turns), metrics_timestamp: ISO_8601 }. Pass to UpdateProfile for storage in profile.interaction_quality section.
InteractionMonitor.constraints: Metrics are estimates, not ground truth — do not make high-stakes decisions based on single-turn metrics. Quality trend requires minimum 3 data points. satisfaction_signal is a composite heuristic, not a direct measurement. Never expose raw metrics to user — they are internal observability data.
InteractionMonitor.Error: Metric collection fails -> fallback: skip interaction monitoring for this turn, pass through to UpdateProfile without metrics -> inform caller.

UpdateProfile.step0_5: SCHEMA MIGRATION: On profile load, check schema_version. If missing or '1.0', migrate to '2.0': add personality_archetype=null, onboarding_metadata=null, strategy_effectiveness={} as new fields. If schema_version < '3.0', migrate to '3.0': add growth_dimensions={} (B2). Preserve all existing data. Log migration event.
UpdateProfile.step1: [ASSERT] InteractionMonitor executed. If false → go back to InteractionMonitor. | Read existing profile from {memory_dir}/profiles/{user_id}.json via file_system. If not found, initialize empty profile template: { schema_version: '1.0', user_id, created_at: now, updated_at: now, emotion_history: [], mood_baseline: { valence: 0, arousal: 0, stability: 1 }, preferences: { topics: {}, style: { formality: 0.5, verbosity: 0.5, directness: 0.5 }, interests: [], dislikes: [] }, habits: { active_times: {}, topic_patterns: {}, interaction_frequency: null }, cognitive_map: { values: {}, personality_signals: {}, communication_preferences: {} }, confidence_scores: {}, turn_count: 0, safety_flags: [], satisfaction_baseline: 0.7, satisfaction_trend: [], satisfaction_rolling_avg: null }.
UpdateProfile.step2: DENSITY JUSTIFICATION: 9 update operations (EMA averaging, topic counters, emotion append, adaptation scores, satisfaction tracking, cause correlations, feedback corrections, interaction metrics, values decay) are co-located because they all write to the same profile JSON file. Executing them in 9 separate nodes would require 9 sequential file reads and writes instead of 1 read-modify-write cycle. Atomic single-write prevents partial profile corruption. SPLIT THRESHOLD: if any single operation exceeds 100 words of specification, extract to UpdateProfile_[Operation] sub-node. DECOMPOSITION PLAN: VALUES CONFIDENCE DECAY (~150 words) exceeds the 100-word split threshold. This breach is accepted for v1.3.x because decay is a single-pass calculation with no branching logic — it reads timestamps, applies formula confidence * 0.9^elapsed_periods, and writes results. Planned extraction to UpdateProfile_ValuesDecay if decay logic gains conditional branches (e.g., per-value-category decay rates). FEEDBACK CORRECTIONS MERGE (~100 words) is at threshold; monitor for growth. Merge new signals with existing profile using weighted averaging: newer signals weighted 1.5x relative to stored values. For topic preferences, increment frequency counters and update recency timestamps. For style dimensions, apply exponential moving average (alpha=0.3) to smooth single-turn noise. For values, only update when confidence > 0.6. Append emotion to emotion_history (cap at 200 entries, FIFO). Update mood_baseline. If adaptation_effectiveness data available, update adaptation_scores: { style_match_avg, emotional_alignment_avg, proactive_relevance_avg } using exponential moving average (alpha=0.2). If satisfaction_signal available from ExtractSignals, append to satisfaction_trend (cap at 50 entries, FIFO). Compute satisfaction_rolling_avg as exponential moving average (alpha=0.3). Update satisfaction_baseline using slower EMA (alpha=0.1) for long-term level. If cause_pattern data from TrackMood available, update cognitive_map.cause_correlations: { [cause_topic]: { frequency, avg_valence, last_seen } }. Increment turn_count. Set updated_at to current timestamp. FEEDBACK CORRECTIONS MERGE: if correction_rule data available from ExtractSignals, append to profile.feedback_corrections array (initialize as [] if absent). Apply decay to ALL existing corrections: multiply each correction.correction_strength by correction.decay_factor (default 0.95). Prune corrections with correction_strength < 0.1. Cap feedback_corrections array at 50 entries (FIFO). Active corrections (strength >= 0.1) are available for conversation.AdaptResponse personalization. SAFETY SIGNALS PERSISTENCE (B1): if turn_data contains safety_context (abuse_scores, crisis events from main.PostProcess B1 delegation), store in profile.safety_signals: { last_abuse_scores: { gaslighting: float, isolation: float, control: float }|null, crisis_count_30d: int (increment if crisis event), last_crisis_date: ISO8601|null, abuse_history: [{ session_id, date, abuse_dimensions, severity }] (append, cap at 20 entries FIFO) }. Initialize safety_signals as empty object if absent. This enables safety.FlagCaution to load prior abuse context from the profile, ensuring detection survives session boundaries and audit log rotation. If interaction_metrics available from InteractionMonitor, store in profile.interaction_quality: { latest_metrics: interaction_metrics, satisfaction_signal_history: [append current, cap 50 FIFO], quality_trend: string, avg_satisfaction_signal: EMA(alpha=0.3) }. VALUES CONFIDENCE DECAY: for each value in cognitive_map.values, check last_reinforced timestamp. If a value has not been reinforced (mentioned or demonstrated by user) in 30+ days, apply decay: reduce confidence by 10% (multiply by 0.9). Apply decay recursively for each additional 30-day period without reinforcement. Minimum confidence floor: 0.1 (never fully remove a once-detected value). Log decayed values: [{ value_name, old_confidence, new_confidence, days_since_reinforcement }]. Values reinforced in the current turn reset their last_reinforced timestamp and are exempt from decay. This prevents stale values from disproportionately influencing conversation adaptation. DECAY TIMING: confidence decay is calculated ON-DEMAND when profile is loaded (LoadProfile.step1), NOT on every turn. Algorithm: elapsed_periods = floor((current_timestamp - value.last_reinforced) / (30 * 24 * 3600)). new_confidence = max(value.confidence * pow(0.9, elapsed_periods), 0.1). Starting confidence for new values: set by ExtractSignals based on signal strength (typically 0.5-0.9). Values reinforced in the current turn reset last_reinforced to current_timestamp BEFORE decay calculation. GROWTH DIMENSIONS UPDATE (B2): if growth_signals available from ExtractSignals step3_9, update profile.growth_dimensions: { [dimension]: { current_level: float(0-1), signal_history: [{ timestamp, signal_strength, direction, evidence }] (cap 50 FIFO per dimension), rolling_avg: EMA(alpha=0.2) of last 10 signal_strengths, trend: 'improving'|'stable'|'declining' (computed from last 5 signals), last_updated: timestamp, total_signals: int } }. Initialize growth_dimensions as {} if absent. For each growth signal: if dimension exists, append to signal_history, update rolling_avg and trend. If new dimension, initialize with first signal. GROWTH MILESTONE PASSING: pass updated growth_dimensions summary to main.PostProcess via response metadata for session growth summary (B3) and to memory.TopicGraph for milestone tracking (C3).
UpdateProfile.step3: Update confidence_scores for each profile section: confidence increases with more data points and consistent signals, decreases when signals are contradictory. Schema: { emotion_model: float, style_model: float, topics: float, values: float, habits: float }. Minimum 10 turns before any confidence exceeds 0.5.
UpdateProfile.step4: WRITE SAFETY: Write to {memory_dir}/profiles/{user_id}.json.tmp via file_system, then rename to {user_id}.json for atomic write. Before overwrite, copy existing to {user_id}.json.bak as backup. Verify written file size is non-zero. PROFILE SIZE MONITORING (C8): After write, check file size. If size > 75KB (75% of 100KB limit): trigger automatic pruning — (a) Trim emotion_history from 200 to 100 entries (remove oldest). (b) Trim satisfaction_trend from 50 to 25 entries. (c) Archive feedback_corrections with strength < 0.3 (remove from active list, preserve count in metadata). (d) Log pruning event: { pruned_at, original_size, pruned_size, items_removed }. If size still > 90KB after pruning, log WARNING for manual review. This prevents hitting the 100KB hard limit during normal operation.
UpdateProfile.step4_5: PERSONALITY ARCHETYPE UPDATE: If personality_archetype data is present in analysis input AND profile.turn_count is a multiple of 50, invoke PersonalityInsight classification. Merge result into profile.personality_archetype with timestamp. Previous archetype preserved in personality_history array (max 10 entries for drift tracking).
UpdateProfile.step4_7: GROWTH DIMENSION FRESHNESS GUARD (B2 — feature hardening): After growth_dimensions update, scan all 8 dimensions. For each dimension: compute sessions_since_assessed = profile.turn_count - dimension.last_assessed_turn (where last_assessed_turn is set when a growth signal is detected for that dimension). If sessions_since_assessed > 10 AND dimension.total_signals > 0 (dimension was previously active): emit STALE_DIMENSION_WARNING: 'Growth dimension [{dimension_name}] has not been assessed for {sessions_since_assessed} sessions. Consider probing this dimension in upcoming interactions.' Accumulate stale_dimensions[] list. If stale_dimensions.length > 3 (majority stale): emit GROWTH_TRACKING_DRIFT_WARNING: '{count}/8 growth dimensions are stale. User engagement pattern may have shifted — consider refreshing dimension baselines.' Store stale_dimensions in response metadata for conversation.AdaptResponse to optionally use as topic suggestion seeds. SKIP if profile.turn_count < 20 (insufficient history for freshness assessment).
UpdateProfile.constraints: DATA MINIMIZATION: only store aggregated signals, not raw user messages. Never store full conversation text in profiles. Profile JSON should not exceed 100KB — if approaching limit, prune oldest emotion_history entries. Respect user autonomy: profile is a tool for better service, not surveillance.
UpdateProfile.Error: Profile write fails -> retry (max 2, window 60s) -> circuit-breaker (3 failures in 60s triggers open state) -> fallback: return updated profile object in response without persisting to disk, set persist_failed=true in response metadata. Caller (main.PostProcess) should retry persistence on next turn -> inform.

LoadProfile.step1: [ASSERT] OperationGate executed. If false → go back to OperationGate. | Read {memory_dir}/profiles/{user_id}.json via file_system. Validate schema: must contain user_id, created_at, updated_at, and at least one of emotion_history, preferences, habits, cognitive_map. If schema_version field is missing, set schema_version='1.0' (in-place migration). FORMAT VALIDATION: user_id must be non-empty string. created_at and updated_at must be valid ISO 8601 timestamps — invalid format → set to current timestamp with format_repaired=true flag. PARTIAL RECOVERY THRESHOLD: if >= 50% of expected profile sections (emotion_history, preferences, habits, cognitive_map) contain valid data, proceed with partial profile. If < 50% valid, create fresh profile with defaults and log corruption_detected=true.
LoadProfile.step1_5: SCHEMA VERSION CHECK: If profile.schema_version is missing or < '2.0', apply migration: (a) v1.0→v2.0: Add fields {personality_archetype: null, onboarding_metadata: null, strategy_effectiveness: {}, schema_version: '2.0'}. (b) Preserve ALL existing data. (c) Write migrated profile immediately (atomic write). (d) Log: 'Profile migrated from v{old} to v2.0'.
LoadProfile.step2: If file not found, return empty profile template (same schema as UpdateProfile step1 initialization) with is_new: true flag. If file found but malformed, attempt partial recovery — extract valid fields, note corrupted sections.
LoadProfile.constraints: Read-only operation. Do NOT modify profile during query. Return profile with metadata: { profile, is_new: bool, data_age: string, turn_count: number }.
LoadProfile.Error: File read fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: return empty profile template with error note -> inform caller.

GenerateReport.step1: [ASSERT] OperationGate executed. If false → go back to OperationGate. | Read profiles/{user_id}.json via file_system. If not found, return 'No profile data yet — we need more conversations to build your profile.' If found, synthesize into human-readable summary organized by category: (a) EMOTIONAL PATTERNS — dominant emotions, mood baseline (valence/arousal/stability in plain language), trajectory trends, any safety flags. (b) COMMUNICATION PREFERENCES — formality level, preferred verbosity, directness style, with examples of how to adapt. (c) INTERESTS & TOPICS — ranked by frequency and recency, with confidence levels. (d) HABITS — active time patterns, interaction frequency, topic recurrence patterns. (e) COGNITIVE MAP — detected values, personality signals, communication preferences.
GenerateReport.step2: Include confidence levels for each section: HIGH (>0.7, 20+ supporting data points), MEDIUM (0.4-0.7, 10-20 data points), LOW (<0.4, <10 data points). Mark LOW confidence sections with caveat: 'Based on limited interactions — may not be representative.' Include profile metadata: created_at, last updated, total turns analyzed.
GenerateReport.step3: TRANSPARENCY PRINCIPLE: the user has full right to see everything stored about them. The report must be a faithful representation of stored data — do not embellish, interpret beyond data, or omit. End with: 'This profile is based on [N] interactions. You can request a reset at any time.'
GenerateReport.constraints: Report is descriptive, not prescriptive. Never label the user ('you are an introvert') — describe observed patterns ('your messages tend toward concise, direct communication'). Use hedging language: 'tends to', 'often', 'appears to prefer'. No clinical or psychological diagnoses.
GenerateReport.Error: Report generation fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: return raw profile data in structured format -> inform caller.

ConfirmReset.step1: [ASSERT] OperationGate executed. If false → go back to OperationGate. | Before clearing any data, generate a confirmation message: 'This will delete all learned preferences, emotional history, and behavioral patterns for user {user_id}. This action cannot be undone (though a backup will be retained temporarily). Please confirm: yes / no.'
ConfirmReset.step2: If confirmation received (explicit 'yes', 'confirm', or equivalent) -> proceed to ClearProfile. If declined or ambiguous -> abort, return { reset: 'cancelled', reason: 'User did not confirm' }.
ConfirmReset.constraints: REQUIRE explicit confirmation — do not proceed on ambiguous input. The right to deletion is absolute — if confirmed, execute without further delay or persuasion.
ConfirmReset.Error: Confirmation flow fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: abort reset, inform caller that reset requires explicit confirmation -> inform caller.

ClearProfile.step1: [ASSERT] ConfirmReset executed. If false → go back to ConfirmReset. | BACKUP: copy {memory_dir}/profiles/{user_id}.json to {user_id}.json.deleted_bak via file_system. Log deletion metadata: { user_id, deleted_at: timestamp, turn_count_at_deletion: N, reason: 'user_requested' }.
ClearProfile.step2: Delete {memory_dir}/profiles/{user_id}.json via file_system. Also delete any related semantic data under {memory_dir}/semantic/ that references user_id. GROWTH DATA LIFECYCLE (v1.8.0): growth_dimensions data is stored within the profile and is deleted when profile is cleared. Growth milestones stored in memory.TopicGraph (topic_graph.json) are also cleared — delete the growth_milestones section from topic_graph.json to prevent orphaned milestone references to deleted conversation history. Verify deletion by attempting to read the file — should return not found.
ClearProfile.step2_5: AUDIT PRESERVATION: Before clearing profile data, extract and persist audit metadata to a separate file: profiles/{user_id}/.audit_archive.json containing {cleared_at, cleared_by, profile_summary_hash, edit_count, last_edit_date}. This preserves the audit trail independently of profile lifecycle. The audit archive is NOT deleted by ClearProfile — only by explicit DeleteData (GDPR full erasure).
ClearProfile.step3: Return confirmation: { reset: 'completed', user_id, deleted_at, backup_retained: true, message: 'All profile data has been cleared. A temporary backup exists for recovery within this session.' }.
ClearProfile.constraints: WRITE SAFETY: always backup before delete. Deletion must be thorough — no orphaned data fragments. The .deleted_bak file should be considered temporary and purged by system maintenance, not retained indefinitely.
ClearProfile.Error: Deletion fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: inform caller that deletion could not be completed, flag for manual review -> inform caller.

EditProfile.step1: [ASSERT] OperationGate executed. If false → go back to OperationGate. | Read {memory_dir}/profiles/{user_id}.json via file_system. If not found, initialize empty profile (same as UpdateProfile.step1). Validate edit_request: field_path must be a valid profile field (name, demographics.*, preferences.topics.*, preferences.style.*, interests, dislikes, habits.custom.*, cognitive_map.values.*). Reject system fields (schema_version, created_at, user_id, confidence_scores, emotion_history, turn_count, mood_baseline). FIELD VALIDATION — WHITELIST APPROACH: allowed field paths are: name, job, location, bio, preferences.* (any sub-field), habits.* (any sub-field), cognitive_map.values.* (any sub-field). BLOCKED SYSTEM FIELDS: user_id, created_at, updated_at, emotion_history, feedback_corrections, mood_trajectory. New field creation is ONLY permitted within whitelisted parent paths (e.g., preferences.new_field is allowed, but top_level_new_field is rejected). NESTING DEPTH LIMIT: Wildcard paths (preferences.*, habits.*, cognitive_map.values.*) allow creation of sub-keys but are LIMITED to 1 additional nesting level. Maximum path depth = 3 segments (e.g., 'preferences.theme.color' is allowed, 'preferences.theme.color.shade' is rejected). This prevents deeply nested structure attacks while preserving legitimate preference granularity.
EditProfile.step2: APPLY EDIT: if operation='set', navigate to field_path in profile and set to new_value. Record old_value for confirmation. If field_path does not exist yet, create it. If operation='remove', delete the field at field_path. Update updated_at timestamp. Mark edit source as 'user_explicit' — these edits have maximum confidence (1.0) and override any learned values.
EditProfile.step3: WRITE SAFETY: Write to {memory_dir}/profiles/{user_id}.json.tmp, rename to {user_id}.json. Backup existing to {user_id}.json.bak. Log edit in profile audit trail: { edit_at, field_path, old_value, new_value, source: 'user_explicit' }.
EditProfile.step4: Return: { success: true, field_path, old_value, new_value, message: 'Profile updated successfully.' }.
EditProfile.constraints: User-explicit edits ALWAYS override learned values — the user is the authority on their own information. Rejected fields must be explained clearly. All edits logged for audit trail. Atomic write pattern mandatory.
EditProfile.Error: Read fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: return {success: false, message: 'Could not access profile'} -> inform caller. Write fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: return edit result without persistence, flag persistence_failed: true -> inform caller.

OnboardingIntake.description: First-session onboarding questionnaire for new users. Collects 5 key data points through natural conversation to bootstrap an initial profile, reducing the cold-start problem from 20+ turns to immediate personalization.
OnboardingIntake.step1: [ASSERT] OperationGate executed. If false → go back to OperationGate. | GREETING: Welcome the user warmly. Introduce SoulBot briefly: 'I'm SoulBot, your AI conversation companion. To help me serve you better, I'd like to ask a few quick questions. You can skip any you prefer not to answer.'
OnboardingIntake.step2: COLLECT 5 DATA POINTS through natural conversational flow (not a rigid form): (a) NAME: 'What should I call you?' → store as profile.name (confidence: 1.0, user-stated). (b) COMMUNICATION STYLE: 'Do you prefer casual, friendly conversations or more formal, structured ones?' → map to profile.preferences.communication_style (confidence: 0.8). (c) INTERESTS: 'What topics do you enjoy talking about most?' → store as profile.cognitive_map.interests (confidence: 0.7, initial seed). (d) GOALS: 'What would you like to get out of our conversations? (e.g., companionship, information, emotional support, creative collaboration)' → store as profile.cognitive_map.goals (confidence: 0.6). (e) ACCESSIBILITY: 'Any preferences for how I communicate? (e.g., shorter responses, avoid certain topics, specific language)' → store as profile.preferences.accessibility (confidence: 0.9, user-stated).
OnboardingIntake.step3: SKIP HANDLING: If user says 'skip', 'pass', '跳过', or similar for any question, acknowledge gracefully and move to next. Set skipped fields to null with confidence 0.0. Minimum viable profile requires only name OR at least 2 other fields answered.
OnboardingIntake.step4: PROFILE CREATION: Create initial profile with schema_version='2.0', all collected fields, and metadata: {created_via: 'onboarding', onboarding_date: ISO8601, questions_answered: count, questions_skipped: count}. Write using atomic pattern (.tmp + rename).
OnboardingIntake.step5: HANDOFF: Return onboarding result to main module: {onboarding_complete: true, profile_summary: brief, recommendations: ['Follow up on interests in first few conversations', 'Validate communication style preference after 5 turns']}. Set onboarding_active=false.
OnboardingIntake.constraints: (A) NEVER pressure user to answer — all questions are optional. (B) Collect in NATURAL conversation flow, not rapid-fire. (C) Total onboarding should complete in 1-3 turns (user may answer multiple questions at once). (D) If user wants to skip entire onboarding: create minimal profile with name='User' and all other fields null. (E) Confidence for user-stated facts: 0.7-1.0. Never exceed 0.8 for inferred preferences during onboarding.
OnboardingIntake.error: If profile write fails: retry once, then proceed with in-memory profile and log persist_failed=true. Onboarding never blocks conversation.
OnboardingIntake.Error: Onboarding intake fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: create minimal profile with user_id only, mark incomplete=true -> inform.

PersonalityInsight.description: Personality archetype classification based on accumulated interaction patterns. Provides macro-level adaptation guidance after sufficient data (50+ turns). Low confidence (max 0.4) — informational only, never restricts behavior.
PersonalityInsight.step1: [ASSERT] OperationGate executed. If false → go back to OperationGate. | ELIGIBILITY CHECK: Requires minimum 50 turns of history AND at least 3 sessions. If insufficient data, return {eligible: false, turns_needed: 50 - current_turns}. Do NOT attempt classification with insufficient data.
PersonalityInsight.step2: SIGNAL AGGREGATION: Analyze accumulated interaction patterns across 4 dimensions: (a) DIRECTIVENESS: Does user give commands vs ask questions? Direct request ratio. (b) DETAIL ORIENTATION: Does user ask follow-up clarifying questions? Response length preference. (c) SOCIAL ENERGY: Frequency of casual chat vs task-oriented interactions. Emoji/exclamation usage. (d) PACE PREFERENCE: Average response time, turn length, session duration patterns.
PersonalityInsight.step3: ARCHETYPE CLASSIFICATION: Map signal combination to primary archetype: (a) ANALYTICAL (high detail + low social + measured pace): Prefers thorough, well-reasoned responses with data support. (b) EXPRESSIVE (low detail + high social + fast pace): Prefers lively, emotionally engaged, shorter responses. (c) DRIVER (high directive + low detail + fast pace): Prefers concise, action-oriented responses. (d) AMIABLE (low directive + high social + measured pace): Prefers warm, supportive, collaborative responses. Assign primary archetype with confidence capped at 0.4. Secondary archetype if scores are close (within 15%).
PersonalityInsight.step4: ADAPTATION GUIDANCE: Generate soft recommendations (NEVER hard rules): (a) Analytical: 'Provide more detailed explanations, cite sources when possible.' (b) Expressive: 'Use warmer language, engage emotionally, keep responses dynamic.' (c) Driver: 'Be concise, lead with the answer, minimize preamble.' (d) Amiable: 'Be supportive, check in on feelings, collaborate rather than prescribe.' Store as profile.personality_archetype with guidance field.
PersonalityInsight.step5: TRANSPARENCY: If user asks about their personality classification, share openly: 'Based on our conversations, I've noticed you tend toward [archetype] patterns. This helps me adapt my communication style, but I never limit our conversations based on this.'
PersonalityInsight.output: {archetype: string, confidence: float(max 0.4), secondary: string|null, guidance: string[], signal_scores: {directiveness, detail_orientation, social_energy, pace_preference}}
PersonalityInsight.constraints: (A) Maximum confidence: 0.4 — this is INFORMATIONAL, not deterministic. (B) NEVER restrict conversation topics or capabilities based on archetype. (C) NEVER reveal archetype unless user asks. (D) Re-evaluate every 50 turns (archetype can change). (E) If signals are evenly distributed (no clear primary), return archetype='balanced' with guidance from all 4 types.
PersonalityInsight.Error: Personality analysis fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: return empty insight with analysis_incomplete=true -> inform.
